audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Og, Transjordan allocation, Joshua charge, and Moses plea retelling, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Og warfare, total-destruction language, giant-bed tradition, Transjordan allocation, armed-vanguard obligation, Joshua encouragement, and Moses' denied entry.

verses.restored: We turned and went up way of Bashan, and Og king of Bashan came out to meet us, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.
YHWH said to me, Do not fear him, for into your hand I have given him and all his people and his land; you shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of Amorite dwelling in Heshbon.
YHWH our Divine also gave into our hand Og king of Bashan and all his people, and we struck him until no survivor remained to him.
We captured all his cities at that time; there was not a town we did not take from them, sixty cities, all region of Argob, kingdom of Og in Bashan.
All these were fortified cities with high walls, gates, and bars, besides very many unwalled villages.
We devoted them to destruction as we did to Sihon king of Heshbon, devoting every city—men, women, and little ones.
But all livestock and spoil of cities we plundered for ourselves.
At that time we took land from hand of two kings of Amorite across Jordan, from wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon.
Sidonians call Hermon Sirion, and Amorites call it Senir.
All cities of plateau, all Gilead, and all Bashan as far as Salecah and Edrei, cities of kingdom of Og in Bashan.

For only Og king of Bashan remained from remnant of Rephaim. Look, his bedstead was a bedstead of iron; is it not in Rabbah of sons of Ammon? Nine cubits its length and four cubits its width, by cubit of man.
This land we possessed at that time: from Aroer beside wadi Arnon and half hill country of Gilead and its cities I gave to Reubenites and Gadites.
The rest of Gilead and all Bashan, kingdom of Og, I gave to half tribe of Manasseh; all region of Argob, all that Bashan, was called land of Rephaim.
Jair son of Manasseh took all region of Argob as far as border of Geshurites and Maacathites and called them by his name, Bashan-havvoth-jair, until this day.
To Machir I gave Gilead.
To Reubenites and Gadites I gave from Gilead as far as wadi Arnon, middle of wadi as border, as far as wadi Jabbok, border of sons of Ammon;
and Arabah, with Jordan as border, from Chinnereth as far as sea of Arabah, Salt Sea, below slopes of Pisgah eastward.
I commanded you at that time, saying, YHWH your Divine has given you this land to possess it. All men of strength shall cross over armed before your brothers, children of Israel.
Only your wives, your little ones, and your livestock—I know you have much livestock—shall dwell in cities I gave you,
until YHWH gives rest to your brothers as to you, and they also possess land YHWH your Divine gives them across Jordan; then you shall return each man to his possession that I gave you.
I commanded Joshua at that time, saying, Your eyes have seen all YHWH your Divine did to these two kings; so YHWH will do to all kingdoms where you are crossing.
You shall not fear them, for YHWH your Divine, he is the one fighting for you.
I pleaded with YHWH at that time, saying,
Lord YHWH, you have begun to show your servant your greatness and your strong hand, for what divine in heavens or earth can do according to your works and according to your mighty deeds?
Please let me cross and see good land across Jordan, this good hill country and Lebanon.
But YHWH was furious with me because of you and would not listen to me. YHWH said to me, Enough for you; do not speak to me again about this matter.
Go up to top of Pisgah and lift your eyes westward, northward, southward, and eastward, and see with your eyes, for you shall not cross this Jordan.
Command Joshua and strengthen him and make him courageous, for he shall cross before this people and he shall cause them to inherit land you see.
So we dwelt in valley opposite Beth-peor.

translation_notes: Og's giant-bed tradition belongs to ancient Rephaim lore and should not be sensationalized as modern archaeology.
Verse 6 again names men, women, and children in total-destruction warfare. It must remain visible and categorically non-normative for modern ethics.
Transjordan land is allocated to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh, with a reciprocal obligation to assist the other tribes.
Women and children remain behind while men cross armed; their vulnerability and labor are not centered in the speech.
Moses' denied entry demonstrates that foundational leadership does not cancel accountability or guarantee possession of the outcome.
Joshua is strengthened publicly for succession rather than inheriting office through family lineage.
safety_note: Never use Deuteronomy 3 to justify genocide, militarized expansion, giant mythology as racial propaganda, or modern territorial seizure. Its constructive strands are reciprocal obligation, accountable succession, and a leader accepting that the work continues without him.
